attempts.strategy: Define the ⊗-invertibility predicate as the scheme-level analogue of Module.Invertible, carrying the inverse witness existentially (so the dual needed by the relative Picard group law is definitional).
attempts.code_tried: def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop := ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
attempts.insight: Designated unit is SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf = 𝒪_X. Resolves the blueprint-reviewer's 'IsInvertible does not exist in the Lean file' concern. Bridge IsLocallyTrivial → IsInvertible still unbuilt (see recommendations).

next_steps: Build the IsLocallyTrivial → IsInvertible bridge to connect tensorObjOnProduct to the group-law engine.

attempts.insight: The single residual must be built from: (a) PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf REFLECTING isomorphisms (PoM morphism iso iff underlying additive presheaf map sectionwise iso); (b) the underlying AddCommGrpCat-sheafification being a localization at J.W (W_iff_isIso_map_of_adjunction / instIsLocalizationFunctorOppositeSheafPresheafToSheafW), plus a compatibility toPresheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ AddCommGrp-sheafify ∘ toPresheaf; (c) the IsInvertible ⇒ sectionwise Module.Flat derivation feeding W_whiskerLeft_of_flat. Also need W_whiskerRight_of_flat (cheap, conjugate by braiding). Est. ~80–150 LOC of sheafification-localization plumbing. Topology J is the small-site one of X.ringCatSheaf, NOT Scheme.zariskiTopology (that probe failed, wrong type).
